item.title: Q4 Ag Credit Survey: What It Signals for Western Cattle Operations
item.description:
Replace with your own analysis.

The Kansas City and San Francisco Fed districts — the two most relevant to western cattle operations — both published their Q4 agricultural credit surveys last week. The headline numbers are mixed, with some improvement in loan repayment rates but continued softness in operating loan demand and a modest cooling of farmland values in drought-affected counties.

Repayment Rates

[Your analysis of the specific survey data]

Pull and chart Fed ag credit data with fredr + ggplot2.

Farmland Values in Drought Country

One notable pattern this quarter: [your analysis of farmland values in drought-affected western counties vs. more favorable areas]

Collateral and Operating Loan Terms

[Your analysis of collateral requirement and loan term changes]

What to Watch in 2026

[Your forward-looking commentary for cattle producers and ranchers]

This post reflects my own analysis and is not an official position of [University Name] or any Federal Reserve Bank.

Questions? Email me.
